verses.text: But we have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the excellency of the power may be of God, and not of us.
We are troubled on every side, yet not distressed; we are perplexed, but not in despair;
Persecuted, but not forsaken; cast down, but not destroyed;
Always bearing about in the body the dying of the Lord Jesus, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our body.

blurb: Paul's words about carrying treasure in earthen vessels remind us that our greatest strength often comes not from our own capabilities, but from the divine grace that sustains us through difficulty. In a world where power seems to shift between nations and empires rise and fall, this passage points to a different kind of resilience — one that endures precisely because it doesn't depend on earthly strength alone. We may be pressed on every side, yet we are not crushed, because the life of Christ works through our very fragility.
